insight: For South Africa, the hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their game momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after the breaks, with a mean change of -0.050 minutes, which suggests that they struggle to regain their rhythm after the brief pause. Specifically, when they weren't pressing before the break, they lost momentum at a rate of -0.050 minutes per minute, indicating that the opposing team may have been able to capitalize on South Africa's lack of intensity. Overall, the numbers indicate that South Africa need to work on maintaining their momentum after hydration breaks to gain a competitive edge in the tournament.
